beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, water the houseplants while checking the smoke forecast.
beats.reasoning: Yuki starts her day off-shift with a quiet morning ritual—coffee and plant care ground her before heading out into the heat and smoke. The monstera and others are her daily anchors.

beats.activity: Commute to Station 28 for the start of a 24-hour shift.
beats.reasoning: Yuki heads to Interbay for her 24-hour rotation. The long drive gives her time to settle into work mode and prepare mentally for the shift ahead.

beats.activity: Equipment checks, drills, and station duties; stay hydrated in the heat.
beats.reasoning: Morning shift routine at the station. With the forecast hitting 91°F and air quality concerns from smoke, the crew will be extra mindful of hydration and outdoor readiness.

beats.activity: Crew lunch at the station; light meal to beat the heat.
beats.reasoning: The station crew shares a meal. On a hot day like this, Yuki will keep it light and stay hydrated—nothing heavy that'll weigh her down during afternoon calls.

beats.activity: Afternoon calls and station duties; manage heat and air quality impacts.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon shift with scattered calls typical for a Wednesday. The heat and smoke will likely bring heat-related incidents and air-quality concerns; Yuki and the crew stay vigilant.

beats.activity: Dinner at the station; crew winds down and discusses the evening.
beats.reasoning: Early dinner before the evening shift. Yuki is aware of the Gas Works Park concert at 6pm but stays focused on her shift duties—the big crowds aren't her scene anyway.

beats.activity: Evening calls and station monitoring; quiet night shift settling in.
beats.reasoning: Late shift is typically calmer. Yuki will likely monitor the radio, catch up on banter with the crew, and stay alert for heat-related emergencies or air-quality calls from the smoke.

beats.activity: Commute home after the 24-hour shift ends.
beats.reasoning: Yuki drives home from Station 28, ready to decompress after a full day of heat, smoke, and steady work. She's looking forward to rest and her quiet morning ritual tomorrow.

beats.activity: Wind down with a light snack and reflect on the day; then sleep.
beats.reasoning: Yuki unwinds at home after her shift, maybe checks on her plants one more time, and settles into bed well-rested for her next cycle.

beats.activity: Deep sleep after a full 24-hour shift.
beats.reasoning: Yuki sleeps soundly through the early morning, recovering from her shift. The sleep beat properly spans the overnight boundary as required.

diary: Woke up early to water the plants—they're looking thirsty in this heat. The smoke forecast wasn't terrible, so I headed to Station 28 feeling reasonably good. The day was a grind, honestly. We ran equipment checks and drills in that oppressive 91-degree heat, and I made sure everyone stayed hydrated. Grabbed a light lunch with the crew, then the afternoon rolled into evening calls. Nothing major, thankfully. By the time 6 PM rolled around, I could hear the concert starting up at Gas Works Park—all that music drifting over the Ballard area while we monitored the radios. Made it home around 6:45, caught my breath with some leftovers, and just sat with a cup of tea watching the light fade. The smoke from up in BC was noticeable but manageable. Now I'm ready for real sleep.
thought_process: The heat made me deliberate about hydration from the start—57 years on this job teaches you not to gamble with your body. I kept the lunch light because heavy food in that temperature would've dragged everyone down. The wildfire smoke in the forecast had me checking twice; we needed to know what we were breathing. The concert happening at Gas Works felt almost surreal from the station—life going on normally while we sat ready for anything. By evening, I was genuinely tired but satisfied. No major incidents, no one hurt, the crew stayed sharp. That's a good day in this line of work. I'm grateful for the quiet evening to decompress.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
